questions.question: What is the equivalent distance of 700 statute miles in nautical miles?
At a ground speed of 184 knots, what will be the time required to cover 288 nautical miles?

While preparing to take a load of cargo to a remote backcountry airstrip in the desert, you observe two steel 55-gallon drums. The freight forwarder has told you that one is completely filled with oil and that one is completely filled with AVGAS, but he can't remember which one is which. The following questions are based on this situation: Your destination has ordered fuel only. In an attempt to determine which drum should go, you decide to use a scale. The first drum that you try weighs 355 pounds, so you decide:

choices.text: that your manual flight computer has dead batteries
to take the first drum that you weighed, since you are believe that it is fuel
that the first barrel could not possibly be fuel
that the scale has an error of 85 pounds

questions.correctChoice: to take the first drum that you weighed, since you are believe that it is fuel

questions.question: Just now you have noticed that there is a label on the second drum that is so faded that you can barely read it. You can make out that it says "Gross Weight 198 kg." How much does the empty drum weigh?

The floor in your cargo area is only approved for a load of 50 pounds per square foot. If you put the drum on a 25- pound pallet that is 36" square, will you even be able to take a 55-gallon drum of fuel?

choices.text: Yes, and there is a 5% safety margin.
Yes, and you have 8 pounds per square foot to spare.
No, you cannot take the fuel because the floor load is exceeded
No, and the floor load limit is exceeded by 16 pounds per square foot.

questions.correctChoice: Yes, and you have 8 pounds per square foot to spare.

questions.question: If you fly at 107 knots for 107 minutes, how far will you have travelled?

choices.text: 220 statute miles Two aircraft are both on final for the same runway at a busy, tower-controlled airport. The controller knows that the first aircraft will fly 144 mph after crossing a point 7 nautical miles from the end of the runway. The second aircraft is behind the first, also on a final approach. Use this information to answer

questions.correctChoice: 220 statute miles Two aircraft are both on final for the same runway at a busy, tower-controlled airport. The controller knows that the first aircraft will fly 144 mph after crossing a point 7 nautical miles from the end of the runway. The second aircraft is behind the first, also on a final approach. Use this information to answer

questions.question: the following questions: If the winds are calm, how long will it take the first aircraft to travel the last 5 statute miles of the approach?

The controller needs to assure that as the first aircraft arrives at the runway, the second aircraft is 5 statute miles behind so that he can make room for a departure. If the second aircraft is assigned a final approach speed of 150 knots when he is 6.5 statute miles behind the first aircraft and 12.5 statute miles from the airport, will he have adequate spacing to meet the 5-statute mile criteria?

choices.text: No, the first aircraft will reach the runway with only 2 statute miles of spacing
No, the second aircraft would need to fly 300 km per hour to ensure spacing
Yes, the first aircraft will land 6.2 statute miles ahead of the second.
Yes, the first aircraft will land 5.3 statute miles ahead of the second.

questions.correctChoice: Yes, the first aircraft will land 5.3 statute miles ahead of the second.

questions.question: Perhaps the pilot of the first aircraft has a rusty instrument scan, and actually flies 10 mph slower than his assigned speed. If the wind picks up to a 5-knot headwind component, what will his ground speed be on final?
If the Density Altitude is 8000 feet and the temperature is 10°C, what is the pressure altitude?
While on an exotic international trip you realize that your fuel order must be placed in liters. Since you need 58 gallons, you request how many liters?
If the OAT is 20°C, the Pressure Altitude is 8000 feet, and the Calibrated Altitude is 8500 feet, what is the True Altitude?

While in a steady climb late at night, you discover a need to know your climb rate. Unfortunately, inky shadows have obscured your VSI. Undeterred, you decide to use your flight computer to solve your problem. But before you do that, what is your ground speed if you have covered 200 nautical miles in just 78 minutes?

With that question answered, you now have time to work on your first problem. The good news is that you started your stop watch exactly 3 minutes and 48 seconds ago as you passed through 1200 feet. Since your climb rate is 520 feet per minute, what is your altitude now- a. Enter your answer on the answer sheet
If the pressure altitude is 10,000 feet and the temperature is -10°C, what is the density altitude?
If the fuel burn is 11.5 gallons per hour, how much AVGAS will you burn in 1 hour and 54 minutes?

If the OAT gauge reads -4°F and you are at 172 knots indicated while cruising at 5000 feet, what is the true airspeed if you know that your thermometer always reads 6°F warmer than the OAT actually is?
How many statute miles are in 190 nautical miles?
If the OAT is 5°F and the Mach number is 0.36, what is the airspeed?
Using a standard weight of 184 pounds per passenger, what is the weight of 32 passengers?
If the airspeed is 163 mph, what is the time required to cover 272 statute miles?

the following information to answer the next problems. In the morning, the wind aloft is expected to be 180@10 knots, and the temperature at cruise to be 15°C. In the evening, the wind aloft is expected to be 230@15 knots and the temperature is to be 5°C. Indicated Airspeed is 138 mph, and fuel burn is 8.5 gallons per hour. You need to make stops in two cities, A and B. The true course from Home to A is 141°. The true course from Home to B is 058°. The magnetic variation is 7° east. In the morning, what would your ground speed be while cruising at 5500 feet pressure altitude between Home and A?
If you were to wait until the evening to fly from Home to A, what groundspeed would you have instead?
If you wait until the evening to fly a round trip from Home to point B and back, you will complete the trip sooner.

If you had been able to fly from Home to A (85 statute miles) in 37 minutes and 45 seconds, you would suspect that the winds were different from the forecast. You would suspect this because your ground speed was:

It turns out that after all of that fuss, you ended up running late and not actually departing from B to Home until noon, rendering your calculations worthless. During that flight, you noticed that you had to fly a magnetic heading of 228° to stay on course. TAS was 130 knots and GS was 112 knots in this case. What were the winds (true direction)?
What is the weight of 9 quarts of oil?
How many inches are in a kilometer- a. Enter your answer on the answer sheet
If you have travelled 284 statute miles in 3 hours and 8 minutes, what is your ground speed?

You find yourself.17" forward of CG. You decide to move a 31 lb. duffel bag from station 54. If the CG limits are 61" to 69" and your weight is 4078 lb., what station will it end up at to bring you to the forward limit?
At a climb rate of 2400 feet per minute and a ground speed of 105 knots, how many nautical miles will you cover during 5 minutes of climb?
How many miles will you cover in 3500 feet of altitude gain?
questions.context: Referenced question 31: At a climb rate of 2400 feet per minute and a ground speed of 105 knots, how many nautical miles will you cover during 5 minutes of climb?

questions.question: Convert 92°C to °F:
If AVGAS consumption is 15 pounds per hour, what is the fuel burn for 1 hour and 14 minutes?
The winds are 299° at 29 knots. On a course of 029° at 129 knots TAS, what is the wind correction angle?
Using the information from the previous question, what is the ground speed?
questions.context: Referenced question 35: The winds are 299° at 29 knots. On a course of 029° at 129 knots TAS, what is the wind correction angle?

questions.question: With a fuel flow of 48 kilos per hour you have an endurance of 6:09 hours. The winds are 098° at 29 knots and your outbound course is 211° with an indicated airspeed of 176 knots. How far can you fly outbound before having to turn around so you don't run out of gas? Your pressure altitude of choice is 8,500' and the temperature is 11°C below standard.
If the airport elevation is 5805 feet and the altimeter setting is 30.57 inches of mercury, what is the pressure altitude?
On runway 6 the winds are 350@24 knots. What is the crosswind component?

After you land your first airline job, a passenger asks you how fast your plane goes. Since you don't want to seem ignorant, you know to account for the difference between your IAS of 225 knots at 10,000 feet on a standard day is actually more like:
You give the passenger your answer, and he says, "Knots, what does that mean to me?" You say "Oh, that's:

questions.question: In return, your passenger replies, "Actually, I'm from Australia and only think of speeds in terms of kilometers per hour." You realize that with his accent, you should have anticipated this scenario. Fortunately, you remember the conversion from the above question. What is your speed in km/hr?
